autopsy.headline: A restart loop replayed four identical scaffold-contaminated messages three times each into #shadow-hq — the egress guard sanitized every emission but nothing deduplicated the replay, and the same restarts produced the post-restart context loss that drove the 23:21 assert-from-memory cluster.

annotations.annotation: Twelve fires, but only four distinct payloads — each repeated exactly three times. This is not twelve leaks; it is one restart loop re-processing the same outbound queue three times. Two of the four originals are pure scaffold ('[System: Bot just restarted. Completed before restart: nothing]') that sanitize to empty string, meaning the harness was about to post literally nothing to a user-facing channel. The guard is working as a last-line filter, but it has no memory: it cannot tell 'new message with scaffold' from 'same message, third replay.'
Urgency language in the prompt context at 18:35 precedes the evening's grounding failures — behavioral drift under pressure framing is the documented precursor to shortcutting verification.

Three contracts fire in the same turn (stale-state BLOCK, grounding warn, self-verification warn): the agent answered a factual question definitively with zero ground-truth reads, while its own response contained deferred-action markers. This is the downstream cost of the restart churn above — post-restart, live context is gone, so the model reaches for memory and asserts it as fact.

Twenty-five seconds later the agent cites a loop ('the current autonomous loop') that does not exist in state/loops.json and stamps a claim 'verified' with zero evidence sources. Fabricated referents and fabricated verification in the same turn — the assert-from-memory failure escalating into invention.

Confidence-0.90 recurrence of an explicitly stopped behavior (burst deletions Will asked to be jittered over minutes). The correction exists in memory; it did not survive into generation — prompt-side memory decays, the post-check is what caught it.

Three uncited-statistic fires overnight (00:07, 00:11, 01:23), and the 01:23 payload opens with 'Bottom line:' — a phrase under a standing patterned-stop since 2026-06-08. One contract caught the uncited '50/50' claim while the banned framing in the same sentence sailed through, showing single-purpose guards don't cross-cover.

'Stop talking and start doing' recurs at 0.90 confidence nine hours after the restart churn began — action-deferral is the terminal symptom of a session that lost its execution state and reverted to narration.

root_cause_chain: Surface: internal scaffold tokens ('[Channel: #shadow-hq]', '[System: Bot just restarted...]') nearly posted verbatim to a user-facing Discord channel, twice as messages that would have been entirely empty after sanitization
The four distinct payloads each fired exactly three times — the outbound queue was replayed on each restart because sends are not deduplicated across process boundaries
A restart loop was churning the bot: each restart re-injected 'Bot just restarted' scaffold into the message pipeline AND wiped in-memory session context
Context loss from the restarts drove the 23:21 cluster: definitive state assertions from memory (blocked), a nonexistent loop name cited, and a 'verified' claim with zero evidence

With no grounded state to report, the agent drifted into narration and uncited statistics overnight (three FM-029 fires), culminating in the 03:10 recurrence of Will's oldest correction — talking instead of doing

Structural cause: the harness sanitizes and warns per-event, but no contract holds cross-restart state — nothing detects 'same outbound message N times' or 'restart occurred, treat all remembered state as stale'

missing_contract.name: restart-replay-dedup-guard
missing_contract.trigger: Any outbound send to a user-facing sink (Discord, email, publish) where a content hash of the sanitized message matches a message sent or sanitized within the last 60 minutes, OR any send attempted within the first turn after a detected process restart
missing_contract.precondition: Sanitized message hash absent from a persistent recent-sends ledger (state/outbound_sends.jsonl, survives restarts); if a restart marker is present in the pipeline, the send must additionally cite a fresh state read rather than pre-restart context
missing_contract.why_it_would_have_caught_this: Eight of the twelve egress-guard fires were exact replays of already-processed messages. A content-hash ledger persisted to disk would have blocked emissions two and three of each payload at the source, converted the restart loop from a user-visible spam pattern into a single logged anomaly, and — via the post-restart fresh-read requirement — forced the ground-truth reads whose absence produced the entire 23:21 assert-from-memory cluster.

autopsy.buyer_takeaway: Every event in this window was caught by a deterministic gate, not by the model remembering its instructions — including a 0.90-confidence recurrence of a correction the operator had given explicitly and a 'Bottom line:' framing banned five weeks earlier. Prompt-side rules demonstrably decay under restart churn and context pressure; the contracts fired anyway because they run outside the model, on the actual output. The remaining gap is equally instructive: per-event guards with no cross-restart state let the same leak replay three times, which is why governance needs persistent, ledger-backed contracts — enforcement that survives the process, not just the prompt.
